data.title: Planet Evening News
data.description: 1. Robinhood excludes some prediction market contracts due to concerns about market manipulation and insider trading risks; 2. X Product Manager: Reduces commission on aggregated accounts to 60%, with another 20% reduction next cycle and cracks down on clickbait headlines; 3. Iranian Deputy Speaker: The Strait of Hormuz is a "red line" and is completely under Iranian control; 4. Justin Sun: Condemns the ongoing token scandals perpetrated by unscrupulous individuals within WLFI; 5. Wang Yongli, former Vice President of the Bank of China: Views US crypto asset policies dialectically; 6. Analyst: Short-term BTC trading is currently difficult; recommends dollar-cost averaging for ordinary investors; 7. Argentina includes cryptocurrencies in the net asset category for qualified investors; 8. Analysis: The probability of the US and Iran reaching an agreement on core demands is not low; 9. CryptoQuant Analyst: If the US-Iran conflict continues to affect inflation, it may force the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates in response; 10. Analyst: Bitcoin and Ethereum are approaching key price levels, which may indicate a market trend reversal.

data.title: STOCKS | Citic Securities Predicts A-Share Market Recovery Amid Improved Conditions
data.description: On April 12, Jin10 reported that Citic Securities released a research note indicating a recovery trend in the A-share market this week. According to Jin10, this improvement is attributed to enhanced market risk appetite, liquidity, and fundamentals. Looking ahead, while the pace of growth may slow, the market is expected to continue its upward trajectory in the short term, with medium-term risks posed by sustained high oil prices. April is anticipated to see a return to fundamentals, with a focus on first-quarter reports and identifying promising industries. Industry allocation should center on sectors with high first-quarter prosperity, marginal fundamental improvements, and those benefiting from policy, low allocation levels, and seasonal demand. Key sectors to watch include resources (gold, energy metals, aluminum, minor metals), AI (optical communication, fiberglass, gas turbines), lithium batteries (battery and lithium materials), oil transportation, chemical raw materials, brokerage firms, coal, general equipment, infrastructure construction, and service consumption.

data.title: American musician G.Love lost 5.92 BTC after downloading a fake Ledger app from the Apple App Store.
data.description: Philadelphia musician G.Love posted on the X platform that while changing his computer and reconfiguring Ledger, he mistakenly downloaded a malicious fake app from the Apple App Store, resulting in the transfer of approximately 5.92 bitcoins within a short period. Chain detective ZachXBT subsequently stated that the flow of the stolen funds had been traced, indicating that the assets had been laundered through a centralized exchange (CEX) deposit address. ZachXBT also questioned Apple's App Store review mechanism, claiming that the platform allows fake apps to be listed, further amplifying the risk to user assets. This incident has once again drawn market attention to wallet security and app ecosystem review mechanisms.

data.title: Two days ago, whale "enai.bnb" transferred 41,025 RAVE from Aster and is now a top 9 holder on the BSC network.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ai Yi, the whale "enai.bnb" bought 46,336.76 tokens on the second day of RAVE's launch at an average price of $0.4556 USD. He held them until 48 days ago, when he transferred all the tokens to Aster (at the time, the price was $0.39 USD), the purpose of which is unknown. Two days later, he transferred 41,025 tokens out of Aster. He is now a top 9 holder on the BSC network. If he had not sold them at that time, his return rate would have been as high as 462%, with a floating profit of $86,000 USD.

data.title: Analysts: Failure to reach an agreement in US-Iran talks will provide further upward momentum for the US dollar.
data.description: Fiona Lim, senior strategist at Maybank, said the market may be somewhat disappointed by the lack of results in the US-Iran talks, but this was not entirely unexpected. The US dollar may gain further upward momentum at the opening on Monday. Some Asian currencies, especially those of net energy importers such as the South Korean won, Philippine peso, Japanese yen, and Thai baht, began to weaken last Friday and may continue to face pressure this week. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iranian negotiating delegation member: The world will witness a new order in the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: According to Iranian sources on the 12th local time, Nabawian, a member of the Iranian negotiating team to Pakistan, stated that the Strait of Hormuz will not be opened "by fabricating stories and Hollywood-style performances." Nabawian stated that the world will witness a "new order" in the Strait of Hormuz. Ali Nikozadeh, Deputy Speaker of the Iranian Islamic Parliament, stated that in the 40 days of war, the United States should have understood that victory or defeat is determined by the will of the people and battlefield superiority, not by empty talk on social media. He further stated that in these past few days, the United States will also understand that diplomacy is a stage for respect, interaction, and acceptance of reality, not a stage for issuing commands. (CCTV News)

data.title: Ethereum(ETH) Drops Below 2,200 USDT with a 1.88% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 12, 2026, 10:55 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Ethereum has dropped below 2,200 USDT and is now trading at 2,198.01001 USDT, with a narrowed 1.88% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: RAVE Token Movement Sparks Interest Among Investors
data.description: Analyst @ai_9684xtpa posted on X about a notable transaction involving the RAVE token. An investor, identified as @EnHeng456 enai.bnb, purchased 46,336.76 RAVE tokens at an average price of $0.4556 on the second day of its launch. The tokens were held until 48 days ago, when they were transferred to Aster at a price of $0.39 per token, with the purpose of the transfer remaining unclear. Recently, 41,025 tokens were moved from Aster, making the investor one of the top nine holders on the BSC network. If the tokens had not been sold, the investor's return rate would have reached 462%, with an unrealized profit of $86,000.

data.title: Analysts: Failure to reach an agreement in US-Iran talks could cause oil prices to rise again, potentially further dampening risk sentiment.
data.description: Charu Chanana, chief investment strategist at Saxo Bank, stated that the failure of the US-Iran negotiations was a setback. For the market, this means the previous easing of tensions is likely to fade. Oil prices may rebound, risk sentiment will be dampened again, and the Strait of Hormuz, even without a complete closure, will remain a real choke point risk. However, this is not surprising given the significant differences in the two sides' positions on nuclear safeguards and the Strait of Hormuz issue. For the US dollar, this means some renewed safe-haven support, but unless there is a new military escalation, a broad-based surge is unlikely. Gold may benefit from renewed geopolitical hedging demand, but the market is not yet fully back to the worst-case inflation shock scenario. (Jinshi)

data.title: Iran's Foreign Policy Focuses on National Protection, Adviser States
data.description: Iran's Supreme Leader's foreign affairs adviser, Velayati, stated on his social media platform on the 12th that Iran's foreign policy consistently adheres to the principle of 'protecting our homeland.' According to Odaily, he emphasized that control over the Strait of Hormuz is firmly in Iran's hands.

data.title: Oil Prices May Rise as U.S.-Iran Talks Fail, Says Saxo Bank Strategist
data.description:
Saxo Bank's Chief Investment Strategist, Charu Chanana, has commented on the recent failure of U.S.-Iran negotiations, describing it as a setback. According to Jin10, this development suggests that previous easing trades might dissipate, potentially leading to a rise in oil prices and a renewed impact on risk sentiment. The Strait of Hormuz remains a significant chokepoint risk, even if it is not completely closed.

Chanana noted that the substantial differences in positions between the two parties on nuclear safeguards and the Strait of Hormuz issues make this outcome unsurprising. For the U.S. dollar, this situation could mean a resurgence of safe-haven support, although a full-scale surge is unlikely unless there is a new military escalation.

Gold might benefit from renewed geopolitical hedging demand, but the market is not expected to revert to the worst-case inflation shock scenario.

data.title: Advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader: Control of the Strait of Hormuz is in Iran's hands
data.description: Ali Velayati, foreign affairs advisor to Iran's Supreme Leader, stated on his personal social media account on the 12th that Iranian diplomacy has always adhered to the principle of "protecting our homeland." He added that control of the Strait of Hormuz is now firmly in Iranian hands. (CCTV News)

data.title: Binance Life's market capitalization surpasses $150 million, with a 24-hour increase of 15.47%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, Binance Life's market capitalization has surpassed $150 million, currently trading at $153.47 million, a 24-hour increase of 15.47%. Odaily reminds users that the price of Meme coin is highly volatile, and investors should participate with caution.

data.title: CryptoQuant Analyst: Long-term Bitcoin holdings continue to grow, signaling a cooling of market speculation.
data.description: CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost stated that the Bitcoin market is currently entering an early stabilization phase, with long-term holding behavior gradually increasing, indicating a potential shift in market structure. Data shows that since October, the number of BTC held for more than a year has increased from approximately 12 million to approximately 12.4 million. While the increase is still relatively modest, it reflects a shift in market behavior towards long-term holding. Since mid-December, the 30-day change has remained consistently positive, indicating that investors are more inclined to continue holding rather than distributing their holdings. Darkfost points out that this change typically signifies a transition from a speculative-driven phase to a phase of stronger long-term conviction. Historically, the proportion of long-term holdings usually declines significantly at market peaks and gradually recovers during bear markets; therefore, the current trend can be seen as an early sign of market stabilization. However, he also emphasizes that this indicator represents a long-term change and typically requires a considerable amount of time to form a clear trend; currently, it is still in its early stages.

data.title: North Korean Crypto Theft Funds Military Expenditures, Experts Warn
data.description: Security experts have highlighted that cryptocurrency theft is increasingly being used to finance North Korea's military activities. According to NS3.AI, researchers have observed a months-long infiltration targeting Drift Protocol, indicating a shift in North Korean operations. These activities have expanded beyond phishing to include attacks on exchanges, wallet services, and key permission holders of DeFi protocols.

data.title: Bitcoin ETFs See Strong Inflows as Market Interest Grows
data.description: US spot Bitcoin ETFs experienced significant net inflows exceeding $786 million last week, marking their most robust performance since February. According to NS3.AI, BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust attracted approximately $612 million. Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley's newly launched MSBT fund garnered around $46 million within its initial three trading days.

data.title: CLARITY Act Urged for Swift Advancement in Crypto Regulation
data.description: Cynthia Lummis emphasized the urgency of advancing the CLARITY Act, warning that delays could push the next opportunity for establishing a crypto regulatory framework to 2030. According to NS3.AI, notable figures such as David Sacks, Brian Armstrong, and SEC Chairman Paul Atkins have also expressed support for expediting market structure legislation.

data.title: Security experts say North Korea's "open" theft of crypto assets has become a means of financing.
data.description: As North Korean hacking continues to escalate its attacks and infiltration of the crypto industry, security experts point out that a key difference between them and hackers from other countries lies in the fact that crypto assets have become a crucial direct source of funding for North Korea's military spending. Reports indicate that North Korean hackers have once again shaken the industry in their recent months-long infiltration campaign against Drift Protocol. Experts state that this model is not merely a "money transfer tool," but rather a direct "predatory profit-making" scheme used to circumvent international sanctions and obtain readily available hard currency. Security researchers point out that unlike countries like Russia and Iran, North Korea lacks sustainable external economic and commodity export capabilities, thus relying more heavily on crypto theft as a core source of income to support its nuclear weapons and ballistic missile programs. Experts also emphasize that North Korean hackers have expanded their targets from simple phishing attacks to include exchanges, wallet services, and key holders of DeFi protocol permissions, and commonly employ long-term social engineering and identity spoofing infiltration methods. Due to the "irreversible once confirmed" nature of blockchain transactions, the crypto industry is far weaker than the traditional financial system in terms of freezing and recovering funds, making such attacks more destructive in terms of speed and scale. Security experts warn that this type of attack, characterized by "long-term infiltration and precise control takeover," has yet to be effectively addressed by the industry. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Iran responds to Trump: Blocking Iran will not open the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: US President Donald Trump retweeted an article on social media on the 12th about a "naval blockade" of Iran. The Iranian Embassy in Austria responded that same day, saying that a blockade cannot "open" the Strait of Hormuz. The embassy wrote on social media: "To those concerned, a simple explanation: a blockade is a blockade. It cannot 'open' the Strait of Hormuz; it will only restrict it. The only way out of this self-inflicted predicament is to remain open and flexible in negotiations. Follow reason." (Xinhua)

data.title: Google News accidentally displayed Polymarket predictions, which the company officially called a system error.
data.description: Some links to Polymarket's prediction markets briefly appeared in Google News search results, displayed alongside mainstream media news, but were subsequently removed. A Google spokesperson stated that the display was due to a system error, adding that "the website briefly appeared in Google News due to an error and is no longer displayed." Reportedly, before its removal, users discovered that Polymarket's market predictions also appeared below reports from mainstream media outlets such as Reuters and The Guardian in event-driven searches; for example, news and betting market information appeared simultaneously in searches related to "whether ships passed through the Strait of Hormuz." (Cointelegraph)
